page: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized server belonging to the Laosv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core density of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It features a massive local storage capacity of 17,279 GB and is Premium Storage capable, making it highly suitable for data-intensive operations. Virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

In benchmark evaluations, the L24aos_v4 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance and high single-threaded bzip3 decompression speeds. Geekbench scores place both its single-core and multi-core capabilities in the top 25%, with notable strengths in image and text processing. Large-block memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small 135M parameter LLM models, though performance drops significantly on larger 70B parameter models due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration.

Given its substantial local storage capacity and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, the L24aos_v4 offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high resource density without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, text and image processing pipelines, and local caching servers.
description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage-focused server from the Laosv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM and a massive 17,279 GB of local storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture and virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V, it supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. Benchmarks show strong single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier, and the lack of a GPU limits its utility for large-scale AI model inference. This hardware profile provides high qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads, making it an excellent fit for large databases, data warehousing, and text or image processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 17,279 GB of local storage, designed for database and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 features 24 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 17,279 GB local storage for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and massive local solid-state capacity.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Massive 17,279 GB local storage capacity with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core and multi-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and heavy I/O workloads
